runs.interpretation: Long-run CES elasticity of substitution between capital and labor in U.S. aggregate private-sector production.
citations: Chirinko (2008) survey/review on the elasticity of substitution between capital and labor
Antràs (2004) on is the U.S. aggregate production function Cobb-Douglas?
Klump, McAdam, and Willman work on normalized CES and aggregate factor substitution
runs.reasoningSummary: My recalled belief is that aggregate U.S. capital-labor substitution is typically below but not far below Cobb-Douglas, with many macro estimates clustering around 0.6–0.9 and substantial specification-dependent uncertainty. I therefore center the median at 0.8, allow Cobb-Douglas (1) as an upper-middle possibility, and leave room for both lower values and modestly above-unit estimates.
